pattern: A sliding window over positive numbers where shrinking from the left keeps the running product below the threshold and each right endpoint contributes window-length subarrays.
hints: All elements are positive, so extending a window can only grow the product and shrinking it can only reduce it.
Slide a right pointer, multiply in the new element, and advance the left pointer while the product is at least k.
Each time the window is valid, every subarray ending at the right pointer counts, which adds (right - left + 1) to the total.

edge_inputs.description: k of one admits nothing since products are at least one
mixed values with several valid windows
single element at k is excluded
all ones with a generous k count every subarray

stress.description: random positive arrays with a mid-range threshold
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, size)
    nums = [rng.randrange(1, 1001) for _ in range(n)]
    k = rng.randrange(0, 1000001)
    return [nums, k]

stress.description: small values with a small threshold to stress window shrinking
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, size)
    nums = [rng.randrange(1, 6) for _ in range(n)]
    k = rng.randrange(0, 50)
    return [nums, k]
